Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, significant proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). These findings suggest impaired renal function, likely chronic kidney disease, with possible tubular dysfunction and poor glycemic control. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Further nephrological and diabetic evaluation is recommended.
